title: PPTP
description: Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol — creates VPN tunnels for secure remote access.
fullExplanation: PPTP (Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol) is a method for implementing VPNs. It encapsulates PPP frames in IP datagrams for transmission over IP networks. PPTP uses TCP port 1723 for the control connection and GRE (Generic Routing Encapsulation, IP protocol 47) for the data tunnel. While widely supported and easy to configure, PPTP has known security vulnerabilities and has been largely replaced by L2TP/IPsec, OpenVPN, and WireGuard.
keyFacts: Built into Windows since Windows 95 OSR2
NSA can decrypt PPTP traffic — avoid for sensitive use
Still used in legacy corporate VPN environments
